title: buildah image: install cpp
body:
Ship `cpp` with the Buildah container images to make sure that
preprocessing .in files works as expected and documented.

Also improve the error message when cpp isn't installed.

Fixes: #3822

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *flouthoc*, *vrothberg* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fbuildah). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/buildah/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [flouthoc,vrothberg] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

> LGTM. @vrothberg would this update `:latest` as well.

Yes, all but the "manual" one are built by Quay automatically. @TomSweeneyRedHat is their loving shepherd.

@vrothberg and probably @cevich, here's the question I don't want to ask.  Do we need to rebuild the current versions in quay.io to make this work for everyone?  Or do we add a note to the README.md telling folks about the problem, which versions of the images were affected, and how (if possible) to fix it for a particular image?
The changes LGTM, btb, unless a README.md adjustment is needed.

> Do we need to rebuild the current versions in quay.io to make this work for everyone? Or do we add a note to the README.md telling folks about the problem, which versions of the images were affected, and how (if possible) to fix it for a particular image?

Oof, maybe both, checking...

...what should happen once this is merged:  Within a day the multi-arch images and manifest list will be built including these changes.  For 'testing' and 'upstream', it will push to the 'latest' tagged images only.  For 'stable', both 'latest' and the version-tagged images will be updated.  Previously pushed (older) version-tagged images won't be affected at all.

So this could cause unexpected changes in the most recent version-tagged 'stable' image.  That's probably where a README update might be nice, making note of the version-tag where the change appears.

Hope that helps.

Ref: https://github.com/containers/buildah/blob/main/.github/workflows/multi-arch-build.yaml
Update: Looking at [the most recent run of the build](https://github.com/containers/buildah/actions/runs/1997346361), it's pushing 'stable' version `v1.23.1`.
Which README are we talking about?  I don't see one where we record bug fixes.  I would keep it simple, merge and then update the images.
If we add to the readme.md, it would be this one: https://github.com/containers/buildah/tree/main/contrib/buildahimage/README.md
